element.name: Silicon
element.summary: Silicon is a chemical element; it has symbol Si and atomic number 14. It is a hard, brittle crystalline solid with a blue-grey metallic lustre, and is a tetravalent non-metal and semiconductor. It is a member of group 14 in the periodic table: carbon is above it; and germanium, tin, lead, and flerovium are below it. It is relatively unreactive. Silicon is a significant element that is essential for several physiological and metabolic processes in plants. Silicon is widely used as a semiconductor material in various electrical devices such as transistors, solar cells, and integrated circuits. It dominates semiconductor applications due to its low cost, excellent electronic properties, and adaptable physical characteristics.

group.label: Carbon group
group.description: The carbon group ranges from carbon and silicon -- the basis of organic and semiconductor chemistry -- through germanium, tin, lead, and flerovium. Properties shift from nonmetal to metal down the group.
